授業方法: This course is a combination of lectures, discussions, pair work, presentations and group work. The emphasis of this course is on small-group discussion in English over a variety of topics that are interesting to the students.

第1回:
【Startup Week】

Implementation method: Online Class (on-demand). Class hours《90 minutes》

This is the introductory video for this lesson and course (VIDEO LINK)

Note: APRIL 10th is ON DEMAND. Our first face-to-face lesson is APRIL 13th
Buy the textbook, be ready for Core Week one.
第2回:
【Core Week 1】

Implementation method: face-to-face. Class hours 90x3 = 《270 minutes》

This week includes goal setting activities and orientation to the course. The textbook for the course will be introduced. The Extensive Reading program (XReading) will be introduced and begin in this week.  

Note: our first face-to-face lesson is on APRIL 13th.

Reading and writing textbook homework.
Fluency homework.
第3回:
【Flex Hours 1】

Implementation method: Online Classes (on demand). Class hours《240 minutes》

Extensive Reading and Media Literacy Unit: Stage One. Stage One begins here.  By the end of stage one, 30,000 words of text should have been read and assessed through the online XReading platform.  Instructions on this process will be given in Core Week 1. 

This unit has a formative assessment after Core Week 4.  Recommended: divide your reading into 3x80 minute or 6x40 minute sessions.
Extensive Reading and Media Literacy; digital interface unit.

Implementation method: face-to-face. Class hours 90x3 =《270 minutes》

This week includes textbook unit 6 study. The writing journal will be introduced during this week. Extensive Reading program (XReading) will be supported with in-class activities.

Reading and writing textbook homework.
Fluency homework.
Extensive reading discussion preparation.

PowerPoint presentation preparation unit.

In this unit, students will asynchronously review their and their partners' slide decks based on a teacher-provided rubric. Meetings outside of class-hours or on Zoom are highly encouraged for presentation practice

トピック.授業外学習2: All students are required to have homework completed before the beginning of class. In general, one hour of lesson time = one hour of homework.

教科書/Required texts1: Charles Browne / Brent Culligan / Joseph Phillips
学位授与の方針との関連Relationship to Diploma Polic: Each class subject is arranged on the curriculum based on the Diploma Policy (DP) and Curriculum Policy (CP) established by each faculty and graduate school. By studying these subjects with an awareness of the DP/CP, students will develop the abilities expected of each faculty/graduate school. The DP, CP, and curriculum table (list of classes, etc.) for each faculty/graduate school are posted in the link below, so please use them as a reference for your study plan. https:/ /kwic.kwansei.ac.jp/cabinet/reference?typeCd=0&cabinetId=557&directLink=1 *Link to kwic (only current students can view)
成績評価Grading0: 評価基準等　Grading Criteria etc.
成績評価Grading1: 20% Participation; 30% Homework; 30% projects and presentations; 20% extensive reading
成績評価Grading2: 備考　Note There are no make-up tests or assignments. Absence on a test day results in a score of zero. Plagiarism, including automatic and AI language generation, will result in a score of zero for the task, and possibly for the entire course.  Late submissions of tasks will be penalised in the following way: Less than 7 days late, or in the following lesson= -50%; More than 7 days late= -100%As per KGU attendance policy, 6 absences result in automatic failure of the course.――――――――出席、遅刻の制度については以下URL先をご確認ください。/ Please refer to the following URL for details regarding the attendance and tardiness policy.https://kwic.kwansei.ac.jp/cabinet/area/detail?areaId=7144&directLink=1――――――――

name: 【200】インテンシブ・イングリッシュ　４６／Intensive English
成績評価備考:
備考　Note
There are no make-up tests or assignments. Absence on a test day results in a score of zero.
 Plagiarism, including automatic and AI language generation, will result in a score of zero for the task, and possibly for the entire course.  Late submissions of tasks will be penalised in the following way: Less than 7 days late, or in the following lesson= -50%; More than 7 days late= -100%
As per KGU attendance policy, 6 absences result in automatic failure of the course.
